candidate_problem_record.subject: math
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in \( \triangle ABC\), \(DE\) is the perpendicular bisector of side \(AC\). If \(BC=8\, \text{cm}\) and \(AB=10\, \text{cm}\), what is the perimeter of \( \triangle EBC\)?
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Given that $DE$ is the perpendicular bisector of side $AC$ in $\triangle ABC$,\\
thus $EA=EC$,\\
therefore $BE+EC=BE+EA=AB$,\\
given $BC=8$ cm and $AB=10$ cm,\\
thus the perimeter of $\triangle EBC$ is $BC+BE+EC=8+10=\boxed{18}$ cm.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集被视为具备较稳定的多解潜力，可进入更强的多解挖掘链路。
